# XCTWaiterDelegate

**Protocol**

Defines methods that are called when [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTWaiter] expectations are fulfilled correctly or incorrectly.

## Declaration

```swift
protocol XCTWaiterDelegate : NSObjectProtocol
```

## Overview

[doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestCase] instances automatically conform to the [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTWaiterDelegate] protocol. If you pass a test case instance as the delegate property of [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTWaiter]’s [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTWaiter/init(delegate:)] initializer, that test case will automatically report timeouts and other unexpected events as test failures.

## Timeout Events

- **waiter(_:didTimeoutWithUnfulfilledExpectations:)**: Invoked when not all waited on expectations are fulfilled during the timeout period.
- **nestedWaiter(_:wasInterruptedByTimedOutWaiter:)**: Invoked when the waiter is interrupted prior to its expectations being fulfilled or timing out.

## Order of Fulfillment Events

- **waiter(_:fulfillmentDidViolateOrderingConstraintsFor:requiredExpectation:)**: Invoked when a waiter is enforcing fulfillment order and an expectation is fulfilled in the wrong order.

## Inverted Expectation Events

- **waiter(_:didFulfillInvertedExpectation:)**: Invoked when an expectation whose `isInverted` property is set to `true` is fulfilled.

## Responding to Expectation Fulfilment

- **delegate**: The delegate to which expectation fulfillment events will be reported.
- **fulfilledExpectations**: An array of expectations that were fulfilled, in order, up until the waiter stopped waiting.

## Inherits From

- NSObjectProtocol

## Conforming Types

- XCTestCase
